meta_title: For Founders: email automation to Solve no coding experience (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to email automation and overcome no coding experience. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Founders: Scale SaaS Email Automation Without Writing Code
content.seo_description: Bridge the technical gap with this intermediate guide. Build automated email systems using Antigravity and Claude Code to drive revenue without hiring developers.
content.answer_block: Overcome the lack of coding experience by leveraging AI agents as your technical co-founder. By integrating Antigravity for rapid workflow orchestration and Claude Code for iterative logic development, you can build production-ready email automation systems that drive customer retention and revenue, bypassing the need for manual development cycles.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS startups is paved with founders who waited for the 'perfect' technical co-founder while their leads went cold. You have the vision and the market insight, but the wall of code keeps you from execution. This isn't just about sending emails; it is about building a high-conversion infrastructure that works while you sleep.

Today, we dismantle the barrier between intent and implementation. Using Antigravity and Claude Code, we will transform your manual outreach into a sophisticated, automated engine. Stop being a spectator in your own product development and start shipping the features that move the revenue needle.

steps.title: Defining the Automation Logic
steps.description: Before touching code, map your user journey. We need to define triggers based on user behavior (e.g., sign-up, abandonment) to ensure high relevance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS architect. Map a 5-step email sequence for a B2B SaaS product, including onboarding, value-add, and re-engagement triggers. Provide the JSON schema for these events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project README.md outlining the email automation workflow and the required environment variables for SendGrid integration.'

steps.title: Scaffolding the API Infrastructure
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate code for your email service, ensuring it is modular and ready for deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a lightweight Node.js service using Express that receives a webhook event and triggers a personalized email via SendGrid API. Use clean architecture.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create src/services/emailService.js to handle SMTP/API delivery logic with error handling for failed requests.'

steps.title: Implementing Template Management
steps.description: Hardcoding emails is a death trap. We will build a template engine that allows you to swap content without redeploying code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a template engine structure where email content is stored as JSON objects. Include a function to inject user-specific variables into the templates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Implement a template parsing function in src/utils/templateEngine.js that supports Mustache-style variable interpolation.'

steps.title: Adding Robust Error Handling
steps.description: Automation failures kill trust. We must implement retry logic and logging to ensure no critical email is ever lost.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add a retry mechanism with exponential backoff for the email sending function to handle rate limits or network failures.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add unit tests in test/email.test.js to verify that the retry logic triggers correctly on 429 status codes.'

steps.title: Deployment and Monitoring
steps.description: Ship your code to a production environment and set up automated logging to track your open and click rates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Dockerfile and a GitHub Actions workflow to deploy this service to Railway or Render upon every push to main.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a deployment.yml file and configure the environment secrets for production integration.'

pro_tips: Never hardcode credentials; always use environment variables and a secrets manager to keep your SaaS secure.
Implement a 'dry-run' mode in your code that logs emails to the console instead of sending them, allowing you to test logic safely.
Use a dedicated transactional email provider like Postmark or SendGrid rather than standard SMTP to avoid landing in spam folders.
Always include a 'kill switch' variable in your database that can instantly pause all automated campaigns in case of a logic bug.
Monitor your API response times; if your automation slows down your main app, move the email dispatching to an asynchronous queue.
content.next_steps: Once your automation is live, implement A/B testing on your subject lines and monitor your conversion metrics to iterate on your copy.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to automate your growth without the friction of a traditional dev cycle. The only thing separating you from a scalable SaaS business is the decision to start. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
